project.name: Foldline
project.audience: fashion students, textile conservators, and material designers

thesis.concept: The page behaves like cloth: it drapes, folds, rests, and finally exposes construction evidence.
thesis.signature: A Verlet textile surface becomes the hero frame and responds to three semantic construction states.
thesis.aestheticRisk: The moving textile cuts across a solid editorial serif composition while all readable copy remains outside the canvas.
thesis.restraint: One fabric, one red seam, no garment model, fashion wallpaper, cards, or ornamental particles.

concepts.name: Draped Page
concepts.signatureInteraction: Choose drape, fold, or inspect while one textile surface changes construction state.
concepts.domainAnchor: A pinned selvedge, cloth tension, gravity, weave, and basting define both the simulation and content hierarchy.
concepts.conversionHypothesis: Letting the page perform construction states makes the notes feel like evidence of a material process rather than a detached article.
concepts.tradeoff: The hero prioritises one cloth sample and cannot represent a broad textile catalogue at once.
concepts.rejectionRisk: Reject if the cloth behaves like liquid, obscures readable copy, or becomes a decorative flag animation.

concepts.name: Pattern Table
concepts.signatureInteraction: Step through paper pattern pieces to reveal seam allowance and assembly order.
concepts.domainAnchor: Pattern pieces, notches, grain lines, and seam allowance determine geometry and annotation.
concepts.conversionHypothesis: A familiar cutting-table ritual lets students understand construction order before opening the notes.
concepts.tradeoff: The tabletop communicates assembly precisely but cannot express the weight and softness of the finished material.
concepts.rejectionRisk: Reject if pattern pieces become draggable decorations without a keyboard-equivalent sequence.

concepts.name: Weave Field
concepts.signatureInteraction: Scrub an inspection lens that magnifies warp and weft without moving the copy.
concepts.domainAnchor: Warp, weft, thread density, and inspection magnification determine the shader field and labels.
concepts.conversionHypothesis: A material-scale inspection moment makes the construction record feel worth opening.
concepts.tradeoff: Microscopic abstraction loses the immediately legible silhouette of a draped textile.
concepts.rejectionRisk: Reject if the shader resembles a generic wave grid rather than a readable weave structure.

selectionRationale: Draped Page makes the requested cloth-like page motion structurally relevant to the archive, offers button-driven state changes instead of requiring pointer manipulation, and hands off from simulation to a provenance-recorded specimen image and semantic construction sequence.

system.geometry: deformable sheet, thread grid, selvedge rule, and staged construction order
system.material: translucent weave, red seam, raking light, and dark workroom

architecture.header: A fixed vertical selvedge rail persists across desktop and mobile; the fixed pause or resume control and aligned study footer occupy that reserved gutter outside the cloth stage.
architecture.heroFrame: A simulated textile occupies and deforms the viewport behind semantic copy and state buttons.
sections: cloth state hero and construction console
ordered construction sequence and return action
architecture.scrollDepth: The simulated cloth resolves into photographic weave evidence, a static four-step construction order, and the shared study-route footer.
architecture.mobileMutation: The rail narrows but remains vertical, the same state buttons drive a lower-resolution one-shot cloth mesh in a stacked hero, and the study footer becomes a two-column route grid inside the remaining content width.

graphics.justification: Tension, weight, fold, and rest communicate textile construction more directly than a carousel or photo grid.

fallbacks.noWebGL: An authored SVG fabric profile replaces the canvas while the construction states, notes, specimen description, and actions remain semantic HTML.
fallbacks.reducedMotion: The cloth snaps to a static selected state; buttons remain usable without a continuous solver loop.
fallbacks.lowPower: The Three.js path reduces cloth segments and weave-texture resolution, caps render pixels, and renders a static selected state instead of running the solver continuously.
fallbacks.assetFailure: Construction notes, weave facts, state descriptions, and actions remain complete if the cloth or specimen media fails.

motion.entrance: Semantic copy, state controls, and a static field render before the lazy cloth enhancement.
motion.nicheMoment: Drape, fold, and inspect alter cloth constraints and rest state rather than triggering a generic reveal.
motion.reducedMotionStrategy: Snap the mesh to the selected construction state, stop the solver loop, and preserve the authored SVG no-WebGL path.

claims.text: The Foldline page labels the textile record as a fictional archive demonstration.
claims.scope: The claim verifies the page disclosure only; construction details are showcase content, not an authenticated museum record.

assets.creator: DEMETA showcase team
assets.prompt: A photorealistic museum macro photograph of one unbranded translucent gray organza sample forming a precise soft fold. Show warp and weft, hand-finished selvedge, stitch holes, and one short vermilion basting line against a mineral-gray table and ink-blue field. Raking side light, wide crop, no person, garment, mannequin, logos, text, glitter, copied designer pattern, or glamour styling.

reports.notes: Three desktop runs per route; the release gate recomputes median LCP, CLS, and TBT against this exact build.
The browser report records complete tab order, every state-control family, pause/resume, CTA activation, visible focus, and 24-by-24-pixel minimum targets.
The browser report records console and page errors for every route/profile pair.
